stemHtml: You are flying a large jet aircraft equipped with fly-by-wire (FBW) at Mach 0.80 when suddenly the FBW experiences a failure leading to manual pitch trim reversion. Why must the speed now be limited to M 0.77?

options.a: The pilots are not used to flying large jets manually.
options.b: At high speed, variation in CG position is no longer compensated by the FBW system.
options.c: The FBW no longer provides flight envelope protection, and the flight mechanics become unpredictable above Mcrit.
options.d: At high speeds, the aerodynamic forces on the control surfaces become too large and could lead to oscillations.
